message:
test(conformance): differential harness for stdio.h snprintf+sscanf vs glibc

CONFORMANCE: libc stdio.h snprintf+sscanf diff matrix.

2 functions but heavy format-spec coverage (6 tests):
- snprintf integer specifiers: %d/%i/%u/%x/%X/%o/%5d/%-5d/%05d/%+d/
  % d/%#x/%08x/%.5d (17 cases) incl. INT_MAX/MIN/UINT_MAX
- snprintf string specifiers: %s, |%10s|, |%-10s|, %.3s, %5.3s, %c,
  %%, surrounding text (9 cases)
- snprintf truncation: 7 size caps (0/1/5/10/14/15/20) on a fixed
  format; verifies POSIX rule that return value is the count that
  WOULD have been written + truncated buffer matches up to size
- snprintf float specifiers: %f/%e/%E/%g + width/precision (12 cases)
- sscanf integer parsing: 9 (input, fmt) cases incl. leading
  whitespace, hex prefix, no-match (count 0), multi-arg "%d %d %d"

Per case we compare return value AND exact byte content of the
output buffer (or parsed values + match count for sscanf).

0 divergences observed against glibc. snprintf/sscanf families are
conformant.

Companion to existing stdio_abi_test which exercises the file-based
path; this harness focuses on the buffer-pure subset where diff
testing is deterministic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
